text: Managing a team requires a balance of several key skills. First, clear communication is essential. You must explain goals and expectations so everyone understands their role. Equally important is delegation; you can't do everything yourself, so you should assign tasks based on each person's strengths. Regular feedback, both positive and constructive, helps team members improve and stay motivated. For example, acknowledging good work encourages others, while offering specific suggestions for improvement shows you care about growth. Finally, conflict resolution is crucial. When disagreements arise, listen to all sides and work toward a fair solution. By focusing on these elements—communication, delegation, feedback, motivation, and conflict resolution—you can build a productive and harmonious team.
